steps.title: Use a one-row Desmos table
steps.content: The question asks which equation shows $c=f(0)$ as the coefficient or base. Make the fastest possible table: set $x_1=[0]$, then evaluate each answer choice at that one input. All four forms are equivalent, so they should all output the same value; the issue is which form displays that value as the coefficient.

steps.title: Look for exponent zero at x = 0
steps.content: At $x=0$, the Desmos table gives $33$ for every equivalent form. The value is shown directly as the coefficient only when the exponent becomes $0$, because $(1.5)^0=1$. In choice B, $f(x)=33(1.5)^x$, so $f(0)=33(1.5)^0=33$. The coefficient $33$ is exactly $c$.

steps.title: Eliminate the shifted forms
steps.content: Choice A shows $f(-1)$ as the coefficient, choice C shows $f(1)$ as the coefficient, and choice D shows $f(2)$ as the coefficient. Only choice B shows $f(0)$ as the coefficient. Choice B is correct.
